articles.title: ‘We are not romantically involved’: I’m marrying my platonic best friend of 20 years. Should I leave him my home?
articles.description: “I own my home outright, have no children or other dependents.”

articles.title: U.S. economy had begun to speed up — until Iran peace talks failed and oil prices surged again
articles.description: S&P Global surveys find rising inflation and supply woes.

articles.title: I am a 63-year-old semiretired physician. If I saved $2 million for retirement, should my Social Security become optional?
articles.description: “At that point, they arguably no longer need the government’s retirement safety net.”

articles.title: I lost my job, my emergency fund is running out and now I have to move back in with my parents. What did I do wrong?
articles.description: I’m 38 and thought I did everything right. What should I do now?

articles.title: ‘I’m still confused’: I sold $80,000 in shares on June 30, so why didn’t I receive my dividend?
articles.description: “To my surprise, the proceeds from the sale were credited to my money-market settlement fund.”

articles.title: Intel earnings show just how dramatically the company has come back from being ‘near-dead’
articles.description: The company is impressing analysts with its profit performance.

articles.title: Pay raises keep shrinking. Here’s how much smaller they’ll be next year.
articles.description: One solution is getting a different job — but that’s not so simple.

articles.title: The 30-year Treasury yield is closing in on 5.2%. A surge to 6% could slam stocks.
articles.description: Long-bond yield spike is increasingly possible — threatening to deepen losses for popular long-term Treasury and TIPS ETFs.

articles.title: SAP’s stock rises on strong revenue, but analysts warn of guidance risks
articles.description: The stock has dropped 40% since the start of the year on concerns that artificial intelligence may entirely disrupt the software company’s business model.

articles.title: Why fixing the housing crisis for under-40s could trigger 10% Treasury yields
articles.description: Bond prices will dive as structural inflation builds, says hedge fund manager

articles.title: Trump's new global tariff draws rebukes from trade partners over forced labor justification
articles.description: U.S. trading partners have rejected the forced labor rationale behind Trump's new global tariffs, while most signaled plans to keep negotiating.

articles.title: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
articles.description: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

articles.title: Trump plans generic drug tariffs from 2028 with two-year delay testing U.S. onshoring push
articles.description: The latest tariff salvo underscored Trump's goal of reshoring low-cost drug production, but displacing established suppliers like India won't be easy.

articles.title: Import prices post surprise gain as costs of goods from China hit highest since 2008
articles.description: Import prices were up 0.3% for the month, as a drop in energy was more than offset by increases elsewhere.

articles.title: World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says
articles.description: The latest edition of the Fed's Beige Book indicated that the soccer tournament wasn't necessarily translating to broader economic growth.

articles.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
articles.description: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

articles.title: Renewed Hormuz hostilities drive ECB rates rethink amid ‘extremely volatile’ outlook
articles.description: Several consecutive days of strikes exchanged between the U.S. and Iran have cast uncertainty on the European Central Bank's interest rate decision next week.

articles.title: China posts slowest quarterly growth since 2022 as investment slumps, fanning stimulus calls
articles.description: That second-quarter growth came below Beijing's full-year growth target range of 4.5% to 5%, the least ambitious goal in decades.

articles.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
articles.description: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.

articles.title: 'Funflation' hits home: Why staying in isn't the cost-saver it used to be
articles.description: Following a wave of price hikes, at-home pastimes like streaming movies or playing video games are pinching pocketbooks.

articles.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
articles.description: Inflation has risen for the eighth straight month in India, as food and energy prices rise due to the Iran war and deficient rainfall.

articles.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
articles.description: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

articles.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high
articles.description: Investors increasingly view China's two-speed growth with robust export and tepid domestic demand as a defining long-term feature of the Chinese economy.

articles.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
articles.description: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.

articles.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
articles.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

articles.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
articles.description: A historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

articles.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
articles.description: Nonfarm payrolls are projected to post a gain of 115,000, according to the Dow Jones consensus.

articles.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
articles.description: Companies added slightly fewer workers than forecast, with hiring targeted heavily toward healthcare-related sectors.

articles.title: North Sea oil and renewables: The UK’s next PM faces a defining energy policy decision
articles.description: Andy Burnham could face an early test over North Sea oil drilling as energy shocks revive the UK energy security debate.

articles.title: Do you really, really love your job? Then you're not alone, according to surprising results from this survey
articles.description: Those surveyed showed a 78.9% rate of workers who "reported feeling positive at the end of their shifts."

articles.title: China widens Japan export curbs, targeting drone makers, nuclear firms and defense institutes
articles.description: China blacklisted four of Japan's government defense research institutes and placed dozens more Japanese firms under tightened export restrictions Monday.

articles.title: Shipping rebounds in Strait of Hormuz one week after U.S.-Iran deal – but fragile confidence threatens recovery
articles.description: Shipping traffic through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz is recovering one week after the U.S. and Iran signed an interim peace deal.

articles.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation plan after first vessel attack under peace deal
articles.description: The pause follows a container ship being struck near the coast of Oman on Thursday, with a U.S. official saying that Iran was responsible for the attack.

articles.title: Trump pledges rapid U.S. response for Venezuela after historic earthquakes kill hundreds
articles.description: The swift U.S. offer of assistance reflects a degree of diplomatic realignment between the Trump administration and the Venezuelan interim government.

articles.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
articles.description: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.

articles.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
articles.description: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.

articles.title: Factory job cuts in June neared financial crisis and Covid levels, S&P says
articles.description: Though the firm's manufacturing index ran better than expected for June, it came largely from an inventory rebuild and despite sharp job cuts.

articles.title: U.S. issues sweeping Iran oil sanctions waivers, unlocking billions in revenue for Tehran
articles.description: The U.S. issued a sweeping rollback of sanctions on Iranian oil, offering Tehran an economic lifeline as negotiations toward a peace deal proceed.

articles.title: Struggling to find a job? Try looking in Nevada
articles.description: The sparsely populated state known for its world-class casinos and dry climate has been a hiring hot spot.

articles.title: Hormuz relief may not ease the economic toll that's already 'baked in,' analysts warn
articles.description: Early signs of reopening of the Strait of Hormuz have lifted the most acute threat to global energy supplies but economic damages from the war will take months to unwind.
